name: post_task
description: Post-Task Checklist enforcement. Triggered after every significant task completion.

nodes.description: Update project_state.md with current stage, new outputs, data changes
nodes.instruction: Update {PROJECT_ROOT}/workspace/project_state.md using Block Memory rules. Hot blocks: ## 當前階段(≤10行), ## 活躍任務(≤15行), ## 資料庫現況(≤10行), ## 活躍知識依賴(≤5行), ## 未解問題(≤5條), ## 主要產出（當前版本）(≤10行). Total: knowledge≤150行, project_management≤200行. Completed tasks → project_history.md (not project_state.md). Conditional: new data ingested→update 資料庫現況, new column meanings→append column_semantics.md, new report→record path in 主要產出.

nodes.description: Check decisions.md for new entries with D-NNN format, ensure sequential numbering
nodes.instruction: If this task created new durable decisions, get the next DB id via `bash shared/tools/conda-python.sh shared/tools/kb.py next-id`, write each one with `kb.py add-decision`, then complete with `{"decision_ids":["D-201","D-202"]}`. If no new decisions were made, complete with `{"skipped":true}`.

nodes.description: Record new rules/findings. Read checklist, report what was learned or explicitly state no new findings.
nodes.instruction:
Read `shared/workflows/checklists/post_task__record_knowledge.yaml`. If there are reusable learnings, write them with `bash shared/tools/conda-python.sh shared/tools/kb.py add-learning --project <PROJECT_ID|system|ai-office|shared> ...` and complete with `{"learning_ids":["STD-L041"]}`. Before writing, check `bash shared/tools/conda-python.sh shared/tools/kb.py add-learning -h` if unsure about CLI flags. `--confidence` must be `high|medium|low`; do NOT pass internal scoring values like `0.90` here. If there are no new findings, explicitly say so and complete with `{"skipped":true,"summary":"No new reusable learning beyond this task."}`. `checklist_responses` is still allowed, but for this single-item checklist `summary` is the compact fast path.
Reference (working add-learning command, do not invent flags):
  bash shared/tools/conda-python.sh shared/tools/kb.py add-learning \
    --id <PROJECT_PREFIX>-LNN --title '...' --date YYYY-MM-DD \
    --content '...' --confidence high --project <project_id> [--related_decision D-NNN]
To list existing IDs before adding: kb.py catalog --project <project_id>
Do NOT use: list-learnings (invalid), export --project (invalid), or bare sqlite3 column guesses.

nodes.description: Evaluate memory snapshot conditions. Create snapshot if conditions met.
nodes.instruction: Snapshot only if any condition is true: ≥3 files written, DB schema changed, report produced, ≥10 conversation rounds, or ≥2 new decisions. If triggered, write `shared/kb/memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md`, run `bash shared/tools/conda-python.sh shared/tools/kb.py import-snapshot shared/kb/memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md`, then complete with `{"memory_conditions_met":true,"snapshot_path":"shared/kb/memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md","snapshot_id":"YYYY-MM-DD","trigger_reasons":["new_decisions"],"trigger_evidence":{"conversation_rounds":12,"files_written":["..."],"db_schema_changed":false,"db_paths":[],"report_paths":[]}}`. Use only these canonical trigger names: `files_written`, `db_schema_changed`, `report_produced`, `conversation_rounds`, `new_decisions`. Otherwise complete with `{"memory_conditions_met":false}`.

required_outputs.description: structured reasons for triggering the snapshot

nodes.description: Verify workspace temp file count is within limits (zero-scatter rule). WARN at >5, FAIL at >15.
nodes.instruction: Scan for zero-scatter violations: (1) _tmp_*/tmp_*/test_output* files (WARN>5, FAIL>15); (2) files directly under projects/<name>/ not in workspace/ or vault/; (3) files under shared/ root; (4) *.backup.* or _backup/ dirs; (5) empty .gitkeep dirs >30 days old. Report count and paths. Clean up found violations.

nodes.description: Update backlog.db: mark completed items done, add new action items discovered during task. Required for project_management type projects.
nodes.instruction: Only for project_management type. Run: bash shared/tools/conda-python.sh shared/tools/backlog.py --project {PROJECT_ID} list. For completed items: bash shared/tools/conda-python.sh shared/tools/backlog.py --project {PROJECT_ID} resolve A-NNN --resolution '...'. For new discovered items: bash shared/tools/conda-python.sh shared/tools/backlog.py --project {PROJECT_ID} add --title '...' --priority P0|P1|P2.

nodes.description: Check if /promote should be suggested (every 3 post_task completions)
nodes.instruction: Coordinator auto-counts. Every 3 completions it appends a [PROMOTE] reminder. No manual action needed unless reminder appears.

nodes.description: EVO-016: Regenerate .md exports from DB (DB is source of truth)
nodes.instruction: If this round added `decision_ids` or `learning_ids`, run `bash shared/tools/conda-python.sh shared/tools/kb.py export all` and complete with `{"exported":true,"export_targets":["shared/kb/decisions.md","shared/kb/dynamic/learning_notes.md","shared/kb/active_rules_summary.md","shared/kb/_index.md"]}`. If no new KB entries were added, complete with `{"skipped":true}`.

nodes.description: Scan newly written KB entries for capability-related semantic relation follow-up reminders
nodes.instruction: If this round added `decision_ids` or `learning_ids`, run `bash shared/tools/conda-python.sh shared/tools/indexing_reminder.py sync-kb --entry-id <id1> --entry-id <id2> ...` for all newly written KB ids, then complete with `{"indexing_reminders_synced":true}`. This does NOT replace kb.py local embedding writes; it only queues capability-related entries for remote semantic relation extraction follow-up. If no new KB entries were added, complete with `{"skipped":true}`.

nodes.description: 自動掃描成熟 learning 候選並寫入升級佇列
nodes.instruction: Informational node only. The actual scan runs in coordinator's Python completion block (not here) and writes results to promotion_queue.json. This optional node is auto-skipped by the engine. The validator check_promotion_candidates runs as a secondary consistency check when explicitly invoked; it does NOT auto-complete this node.
